title: lichen_l2.c:610 lichen_peer_add NULL check redundant with nonnull attr
description: Contract violation: lichen_peer_add() - Function has __attribute__((nonnull)) but also does explicit NULL check at line 621. The nonnull attribute allows compiler to assume pointers are never NULL and potentially optimize away the check. WHY this is a bug: The check may be silently removed by the compiler, then -EINVAL is never returned despite being documented. HOW to fix: Either remove nonnull attribute (defensive coding style) or remove the NULL check (trust callers style). Recommend removing nonnull to keep defensive check.
close_reason: False positive: lichen_peer_add() does NOT have __attribute__((nonnull)). Verified by grep across entire bridge-zephyr codebase - no nonnull attribute exists on this function. The defensive NULL checks at lines 610-612 work correctly without conflicting attributes. The documentation at line 67 correctly states -EINVAL is returned for NULL. No code changes needed.
